# Hex Strategy Map

> A hexagonal grid toolkit for Godot 4.6+. Render-agnostic simulation
> (deterministic, JSON-serializable) plus a bundled 2D renderer. Built
> for turn-based strategy, tactical RPGs / CRPGs, hex-and-counter
> wargames, hex crawls, roguelikes, and hex puzzles.

The addon ships in two tiers. The **free tier** (MIT, distributed via the
[Godot Asset Library](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/5136),
the [Godot Store](https://store.godotengine.org/asset/javier-islas/hex-strategy-map),
and [itch.io](https://dimcairion.itch.io/hex-strategy-map-free)) is the
foundation: hex math, pathfinding, fog of war, rendering, camera. The
**Pro tier** (commercial, royalty-free, on
[itch.io](https://dimcairion.itch.io/hex-strategy-map)) adds units, turn
manager, combat, procedural maps, texture-based fog of war that scales
to 250×250 grids, Tiled JSON import, save/load, a visual `@tool` map
editor, and a 2-player skirmish demo.

The core design principle is **logic / rendering separation**: every
module that drives game state (HexGrid, PathFinder, FlowField, FogOfWar)
operates on coordinates and data only. The bundled 2D renderer is
optional. You can drive the same logic from a 3D scene, a headless
server, or any custom renderer. With a seeded RNG, runs are
reproducible — suitable for play-by-email, lockstep multiplayer,
headless servers, replays, and AI/ML training environments.

All game-specific behavior is injectable via Callables (terrain costs,
visibility formulas, tile visuals, neighbor filters, combat outcomes).
No subclassing, no magic strings.

## Free tier — core modules (MIT)

- [HexGrid](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/hex_grid.gd): offset/cube coordinates, neighbors, distances, terrain & edge costs, elevation-aware line-of-sight via injectable callable
- [HexCell](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/hex_cell.gd): cell model with terrain, tag, metadata dictionary, per-player fog state, elevation
- [PathFinder](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/path_finder.gd): Dijkstra (`find_reachable`) and A* (`find_path_astar`), with injectable neighbor filters
- [HexRenderer](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/hex_renderer.gd): per-hex Polygon2D / Sprite2D / AnimatedSprite2D rendering with textures, atlases, overlays, icons, edges
- [BatchHexLayer](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/batch_hex_layer.gd): 200×200+ maps via single `_draw()` with viewport AABB culling and dirty tracking
- [FogOfWar](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/fog_of_war.gd): 3-state per-player fog (Hidden / Explored / Visible) with LOS-based reveal
- [MapCamera](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/addons/hex_strategy_map/map_camera.gd): follow target, drag, zoom, edge-scroll

## Pro tier — additional modules (commercial, royalty-free)

Source code is not public; the linked Pro API reference describes the
full surface area of each module.

- [MapToken](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#maptoken): unit movement with configurable movement points, path following, signals, serialization
- [TurnManager](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#turnmanager): turn cycle with player phases, interval hooks, signals
- [CombatResolver](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#combatresolver): pluggable combat with injectable damage, terrain bonus, flanking, outcome callables
- [FlowField](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#flowfield): one-to-many pathfinding. Build once, `trace_path` is ~22 000× faster than rebuilding — efficient for groups sharing a destination
- [MapGenerator](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#mapgenerator): procedural terrain via noise, rivers, scatterable locations
- [UnitRegistry](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#unitregistry): centralized unit tracking by owner, coordinate index with auto-sync, stacking rules
- [HexMiniMap](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#hexminimap): minimap rendering with per-player fog and token markers
- [TiledImporter](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#tiledimporter): import [Tiled Map Editor](https://www.mapeditor.org/) JSON maps directly into HexGrid
- [SaveManager](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#savemanager): JSON-based save/load slot management for grid + units + fog + turn state
- [FogTextureRenderer](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#fogtexturerenderer) *(new in 1.4.0)*: texture-based fog of war for 250×250 grids. Single quad + shader sampling a visibility texture; bilinear smoothing for continuous fog instead of hexagonal holes. Incremental `update_cell` is ~84 000× faster than the full sweep
- [HexMapNode](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md#hexmapnode): `@tool` Node2D for painting terrain visually in the Godot editor with auto-serialization

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/README.md): top-level overview, quick start, signals reference
- [Getting started](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/getting_started.md): tutorial for the free tier
- [API reference (free)](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/api_reference.md): free-tier API
- [API reference (Pro)](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/pro_api_reference.md): Pro-tier API
- [Customization guide](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/customization.md): how to inject terrain costs, visibility formulas, tile visuals, combat rules
- [Benchmarks](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free/blob/main/docs/benchmarks.md): reference numbers per module, hardware notes, guide on choosing between A*, cached Dijkstra and FlowField

## Where to get it

- Free tier (MIT):
  - [Godot Asset Library](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/5136)
  - [Godot Store](https://store.godotengine.org/asset/javier-islas/hex-strategy-map)
  - [itch.io free](https://dimcairion.itch.io/hex-strategy-map-free)
  - [GitHub mirror](https://github.com/JavierIslas/hex-strategy-map-free) — source of the distributed free package
- Pro tier:
  - [itch.io](https://dimcairion.itch.io/hex-strategy-map) — one-time purchase, commercial license, unlimited projects, no royalties

## Not for

This addon is intentionally narrow. It is **not** the right choice for:

- **Square or isometric grids** — use Godot's built-in `TileMap`
- **Free-form 3D navigation on meshes** — use `NavigationServer3D`
- **A full RPG framework** — provides the tactical / map layer only. Stats, inventory, dialogue, quests, UI are your game's responsibility
- **Real-time action games** — designed around turn-based and step-based simulation. The grid model assumes discrete state changes, not continuous motion

## Requirements

- Godot 4.6+
- No external dependencies (pure GDScript)
- 269 automated tests (free) / 598 automated tests (full) via gdUnit4
